name: BTLE
summary: BTLE is a free and open-source Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Software Defined Radio software suite and [open BTLE chip design](https://github.com/JiaoXianjun/BTLE/tree/master/open_btle_baseband_chip).

documentation.readme: BTLE is a free and open-source Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Software Defined Radio software suite and open BTLE chip design.
usage_sections: BTLE baseband algorithms description, Python and Verilog implementation, FPGA and SkyWater 130 PDK (OpenLane2 workflow) results.
Analog Devices AD9361 + Xilinx Zynq FPGA implementation: FPGA Bluetooth
btlerx - BLE sniffer. Besides sniff broadcasting/fixed channel, it can also track channel hopping of a communication link.
PHY and upper layer are implemented in software (C language). Full Software Defined Radio Flexibility.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: BTLE is a free and open-source Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Software Defined Radio software suite and [open BTLE chip design](https://github.com/JiaoXianjun/BTLE/tree/master/open_btle_baseband_chip).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: BTLE is a free and open-source Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Software Defined Radio software suite and open BTLE chip design.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/JiaoXianjun/BTLE.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd BTLE/host`. Further invocations from upstream: `cmake ../                   (default. for HackRF)`; `cmake ../ -DUSE_BLADERF=1   (only for bladeRF)`. BTLE is a free and open-source Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Software Defined Radio software suite and open BTLE chip design.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
